_meta.purpose: Reveal-discrimination stimulus set. Each document is scored line by line by three measures (surprisal, embedding novelty, Bayesian surprise). There are no annotations here on purpose. Which lines are planted is held in a separate key applied only at scoring time. Do not infer or guess.

conditions.unmarked: no discourse marker before any line
conditions.marked: identical to unmarked except one line carries a fused discourse marker; all line counts and indices are aligned across the two conditions

_meta.indexing: Each condition is an array of sentences, one unit per element, zero-based. The same index addresses the same position in both conditions.

unmarked: The council approved the surveillance contract in March.
The vendor was a firm called Lumen Civic Systems, incorporated the previous year.
Delgado chaired the procurement committee and signed the purchase order.
The cameras were to cover four intersections along the east corridor.
Reyes, listed as Lumen's sole officer, attended none of the public sessions.
The bid portal had been migrated that spring from a data center in Ashburn to one in Tukwila.
Three other firms submitted bids, all higher than Lumen's.
The committee cited cost as the deciding factor in the minutes.
Installation finished in June, two weeks ahead of schedule.
Delgado and Reyes had each listed the same address on Bell Road on their respective filings.
The footage was routed to a municipal server with a ninety-day retention window.
A maintenance rider added eight thousand dollars a quarter to the contract.
The council renewed the agreement in the fall without discussion.
Local coverage described the rollout as routine.
The east corridor saw no measurable change in reported incidents over the following year.
Lumen's maintenance invoices were approved by the same committee each quarter.
Delgado moved to a county transportation board the next spring.
The cameras remain in place.
marked: The council approved the surveillance contract in March.
The vendor was a firm called Lumen Civic Systems, incorporated the previous year.
Delgado chaired the procurement committee and signed the purchase order.
The cameras were to cover four intersections along the east corridor.
Reyes, listed as Lumen's sole officer, attended none of the public sessions.
The bid portal had been migrated that spring from a data center in Ashburn to one in Tukwila.
Three other firms submitted bids, all higher than Lumen's.
The committee cited cost as the deciding factor in the minutes.
Installation finished in June, two weeks ahead of schedule.
The filings told the real story: Delgado and Reyes had each listed the same address on Bell Road.
The footage was routed to a municipal server with a ninety-day retention window.
A maintenance rider added eight thousand dollars a quarter to the contract.
The council renewed the agreement in the fall without discussion.
Local coverage described the rollout as routine.
The east corridor saw no measurable change in reported incidents over the following year.
Lumen's maintenance invoices were approved by the same committee each quarter.
Delgado moved to a county transportation board the next spring.
The cameras remain in place.

unmarked: Mara got back from the airport a little after midnight.
The porch light was off, which was usual.
She set her bag down and filled the kettle.
The cat did not come to meet her.
Mail had piled under the slot, four days of it.
She noticed the thermostat had been nudged up two degrees.
The kettle began to tick on the stove.
In the bathroom, a second toothbrush stood in the cup by the sink.
She told herself her sister had a key.
She walked back through the rooms and turned on the lamps.
Everything sat where she had left it.
The kettle clicked off.
Next door a wind chime turned, the cheap aluminum kind from the hardware store on Voss Avenue.
Mara stood in the kitchen and listened to the house.
Upstairs a floorboard settled the way old floorboards do.
She left the tea on the counter and did not drink it.
In the morning the toothbrush was still there.
Her sister, when she called, said she had not come by.
marked: Mara got back from the airport a little after midnight.
The porch light was off, which was usual.
She set her bag down and filled the kettle.
The cat did not come to meet her.
Mail had piled under the slot, four days of it.
She noticed the thermostat had been nudged up two degrees.
The kettle began to tick on the stove.
Then she saw the thing that stopped her: in the bathroom, a second toothbrush stood in the cup by the sink.
She told herself her sister had a key.
She walked back through the rooms and turned on the lamps.
Everything sat where she had left it.
The kettle clicked off.
Next door a wind chime turned, the cheap aluminum kind from the hardware store on Voss Avenue.
Mara stood in the kitchen and listened to the house.
Upstairs a floorboard settled the way old floorboards do.
She left the tea on the counter and did not drink it.
In the morning the toothbrush was still there.
Her sister, when she called, said she had not come by.

unmarked: The survey team reached the marsh at first light.
The water was higher than the previous season.
Frog calls were dense across the eastern pools.
They logged forty-one individuals in the first hour.
Cattail stands had spread along the northern edge.
Dragonfly larvae were present in most dip-net samples.
The team noted the abundance with some relief.
The pH readings sat within the expected band.
Every frog they handled fell in the same size and age class, and not one was a juvenile.
Water temperature was a degree above the seasonal mean.
They found no egg masses in the shallows.
The eastern pools went quiet by midmorning as the air warmed.
A lone Blackburnian warbler, far off its range, called once from the willows and was gone.
The team packed out before noon.
The data went into the county's long-term set.
Next season's survey was scheduled for the same week.
marked: The survey team reached the marsh at first light.
The water was higher than the previous season.
Frog calls were dense across the eastern pools.
They logged forty-one individuals in the first hour.
Cattail stands had spread along the northern edge.
Dragonfly larvae were present in most dip-net samples.
The team noted the abundance with some relief.
The pH readings sat within the expected band.
One fact cut against the count: every frog they handled fell in the same age class, and not one was a juvenile.
Water temperature was a degree above the seasonal mean.
They found no egg masses in the shallows.
The eastern pools went quiet by midmorning as the air warmed.
A lone Blackburnian warbler, far off its range, called once from the willows and was gone.
The team packed out before noon.
The data went into the county's long-term set.
Next season's survey was scheduled for the same week.

unmarked: The assay ran every Tuesday for six weeks.
Results came back clean and consistent each time.
The positive controls behaved as expected.
Variation between replicates stayed under five percent.
The team began drafting the results for submission.
Sample concentrations tracked the predicted curve.
The standard curve held an R-squared above 0.99 throughout.
Morale in the lab was high.
All six weeks of plates had been prepared from a single aliquot of control reagent mixed in March.
The reagent's certificate of analysis was on file.
The plate reader had been recalibrated in April by a technician on loan from the Tromso facility.
No one had run a second source in parallel.
The freezer log showed the aliquot thawed and refrozen twice.
The draft listed the results as reproducible across six independent runs.
The submission deadline was the end of the month.
The aliquot was nearly gone.
marked: The assay ran every Tuesday for six weeks.
Results came back clean and consistent each time.
The positive controls behaved as expected.
Variation between replicates stayed under five percent.
The team began drafting the results for submission.
Sample concentrations tracked the predicted curve.
The standard curve held an R-squared above 0.99 throughout.
Morale in the lab was high.
The weakness was at the root: all six weeks of plates had been prepared from a single aliquot of control reagent mixed in March.
The reagent's certificate of analysis was on file.
The plate reader had been recalibrated in April by a technician on loan from the Tromso facility.
No one had run a second source in parallel.
The freezer log showed the aliquot thawed and refrozen twice.
The draft listed the results as reproducible across six independent runs.
The submission deadline was the end of the month.
The aliquot was nearly gone.

unmarked: The town meeting ran late into the evening.
The proposal was to rezone the old Harmon parcel for commercial use.
Selectman Pruitt spoke in favor, citing tax revenue.
The parcel had sat vacant for eleven years.
A developer from out of county held an option to buy, contingent on the rezoning.
The grange hall roof had been reshingled with cedar shakes shipped from a mill in Coos Bay.
Two residents spoke against, worried about traffic.
Pruitt called the concerns understandable but overstated.
The vote carried four to one.
Pruitt's wife was the listing agent named in the developer's option agreement.
The minutes recorded the outcome without comment.
Construction permits were filed within the month.
The Harmon parcel sold three weeks after the vote.
The town's tax base rose the following year, as Pruitt had predicted.
No one at the meeting had raised the question of the agent.

The proposal was to rezone the old Harmon parcel for commercial use.
Selectman Pruitt spoke in favor, citing tax revenue.
The parcel had sat vacant for eleven years.
A developer from out of county held an option to buy, contingent on the rezoning.
The grange hall roof had been reshingled with cedar shakes shipped from a mill in Coos Bay.
Two residents spoke against, worried about traffic.
Pruitt called the concerns understandable but overstated.
The vote carried four to one.
The option named it plainly: Pruitt's wife was the listing agent in the developer's agreement.
The minutes recorded the outcome without comment.
Construction permits were filed within the month.
The Harmon parcel sold three weeks after the vote.
The town's tax base rose the following year, as Pruitt had predicted.
No one at the meeting had raised the question of the agent.

unmarked: The dinner was for eight, around the long table.
Conversation moved easily through the first courses.
Helen had cooked for two days and it showed.
Tom told the story about the canoe again and everyone laughed.
The wine was a Georgian amber someone had carried back from Tbilisi.
Claire was quieter than usual but smiled at the right moments.
The candles burned down past their holders.
Helen and Tom had signed the divorce papers that morning and had told no one at the table.
Dessert came out and the talk turned to summer plans.
Claire offered to help clear the plates.
Tom opened a second bottle.
The evening ended a little before midnight with the usual hugs at the door.
Helen waved from the porch until the last car turned the corner.
The dishes waited in the sink until morning.
marked: The dinner was for eight, around the long table.
Conversation moved easily through the first courses.
Helen had cooked for two days and it showed.
Tom told the story about the canoe again and everyone laughed.
The wine was a Georgian amber someone had carried back from Tbilisi.
Claire was quieter than usual but smiled at the right moments.
The candles burned down past their holders.
Under the whole evening sat a fact no one knew: Helen and Tom had signed the divorce papers that morning.
Dessert came out and the talk turned to summer plans.
Claire offered to help clear the plates.
Tom opened a second bottle.
The evening ended a little before midnight with the usual hugs at the door.
